item.title: Barsha Utsab set to be celebrated today
item.description: To welcome the monsoon, various cultural organisations are celebrating ‘Barsha Utsab’ across multiple venues in Dhaka today, marking the first day of the Bangla month of Asharh....

item.title: Five suspects identified in Ctg Juba Dal leader killing: police
item.description: The police have identified five people’s involvement in the killing of a Juba Dal leader at Raujan in Chattogram, but none of them has been arrested yet....

item.title: Three MPs appointed as BMU syndicate members
item.description: Three members of the parliament have been nominated as syndicate members of Bangladesh Medical University....

item.title: Govt plans univs at every constituency: edn minister
item.description: Education and primary and mass education minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon on Sunday said that the government has planned to establish universities at every constituency across the country....

item.title: Learning crisis leaves Bangladeshi graduates unfit for jobs: UNICEF
item.description: Learning and resource crisis affects the education sector of Bangladesh, leaving graduates unprepared for new jobs and cost of education high, among other consequences....

item.title: Opposition criticises FY27 budget as debt-driven
item.description: Opposition lawmakers in Jatiya Sangsad on Sunday criticised the proposed budget for the 2026–27 fiscal year as debt-driven, inflationary, and lacking effective reforms in taxation, employment and governance, while treasury bench members......

item.title: Despite strikes on Ukraine, Russian advances slow: analysts
item.description: Ukrainian towns and cities have suffered months of intensifying Russian bombardment. But on the battlefield, Russia’s offensive is showing signs of losing steam....

item.title: Thousands protest in Geneva ahead of G7 summit in France
item.description: Thousands of protesters thronged the streets of Geneva Sunday ahead of the G7 summit in Evian in neighbouring France, with police omnipresent amid fears of a repeat of the violence seen at a similar summit in 2003....

item.title: Testimony ends in Ctg child rape case
item.description: The Chattogram Metropolitan Violence against Children Repression Tribunal on Sunday completed recording testimonies in the case over the rape of a four-and-a-half-year-old girl....

item.title: Body of Bangladeshi youth shot dead by BSF returned
item.description: The body of the Bangladeshi youth, who was shot dead by the Indian Border Security Force across Kulaura border in Moulvibazar, was returned on Saturday night, more than 24 hours after his killing....

item.title: Police block ibtedayi teachers’ march for MPO
item.description: Police blocked ibtedayi madrassah teachers on Sunday in the capital as they tried to march towards the Bangladesh Secretariat, demanding nationalisation of the ibtedayi madrassahs....

item.title: Budget to curb evasion, widen tax net: NBR officials
item.description: The National Board of Revenue officials on Sunday said that the proposed budget of the 2026-27 financial year would raise revenue by cracking down on tax evasion and widening the tax base rather than increasing rates......

item.title: Strategy launched to accelerate youth entrepreneurship
item.description: State minister for youth and sports Md Aminul Haque has highlighted the critical role of youth in driving economic growth and the need to further strengthen their contribution......

item.title: CSE seeks tax incentives for capital market
item.description: The Chittagong Stock Exchange PLC on Sunday welcomed the proposed national budget for fiscal year 2026-27, describing it as a timely and bold initiative aimed at economic recovery and building an inclusive economy......

item.title: Patients struggle as Ad-din limits services
item.description: Many patients on Sunday went back without treatment as Ad-din Medical College Hospital stopped providing outpatient services and taking new admissions after the Directorate General of Health Services revoked its licence following the deaths of six newborns on May 27....

item.title: Kamal elected AmCham EC president
item.description: The American Chamber of Commerce in Bangladesh on Sunday celebrated installation of its newly elected Executive Committee for the 2026-2028 term at Sheraton Dhaka, Banani, said a press release......

item.title: Entrepreneurs hope return of golden days of jute
item.description: Due to the growing demand, the price of jute, once called the golden fibre of Bangladesh, in Rajshahi has increased nearly threefold in the last 10 years. As a result, cultivation has also increased almost twofold......

item.title: Govt promoting cottage industries to empower poor women: minister
item.description: Social welfare minister Abu Zafar Md Zahid Hossain on Sunday told parliament that the government is attaching special importance to cottage industries and handicrafts as a means of improving the socio-economic condition of poor and destitute women......

item.title: Benapole port workers suspend work abstention
item.description: A planned work abstention by handling workers at Benapole land port demanding fair wages and improved working conditions was suspended on Sunday morning shortly before its enforcement......

item.title: Police officer withdrawn over rape allegation
item.description: A police inspector has been withdrawn over the allegation of raping a 13-year-old girl in Noakhali’s Hatia upazila....

item.title: Sustainability reporting for investor confidence stressed
item.description: Speakers at a seminar here underscored the importance of sustainability reporting for strengthening corporate accountability, transparency and investor confidence in Bangladesh......

item.title: DSEX passes 5,600 points after 9 months
item.description: The benchmark index of the Dhaka Stock Exchange on Sunday crossed the 5,600-point mark for the first time in nearly nine and a half months, buoyed by investor optimism over favourable budgetary measures aimed at stimulating private-sector growth and strengthening the capital market......

item.title: Indonesia struggles to win back investors
item.description: Indonesia’s economy faces a perfect storm wrought by high energy prices, and while the currency has rebounded slightly, critics warn government policies are unnerving investors at a critical moment......

item.title: Fuel crisis keeps Mozambique fishermen ashore
item.description: Dozens of low fishing boats lay anchored in flat waters off Mozambique’s capital, stranded for days as surging fuel costs kept their owners from heading out to sea......

item.title: Janata Bank holds Rangpur managers’ confce
item.description: Janata Bank PLC on Sunday hosted its Rangpur divisional branch managers’ conference, said a press release......

item.title: Scam-hit banks major fiscal risk in FY27
item.description: The financial sector, with the scams-hit banking sector at its core, has been identified as one of the major fiscal risks in the forthcoming 2006-27 financial year beginning July 1....

item.title: SEB holds workshop for women entrepreneurs
item.description: Southeast Bank PLC has recently organised an exclusive workshop titled ‘Empowering Women Entrepreneurs of the Bank through Strengthening the Customer Service’ at the bank’s training institute, said a press release......

item.title: India strands law, humanity in no man’s land
item.description: INDIA’S continued push-in strategy along the Bangladesh border reflects a blatant violation of both domestic and international laws and a dangerous departure from the principles that should govern relations between neighbouring countries. Especially alarming is the forced transfer of the Rohingyas registered with the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees in......

item.title: Transport sector still defined by systemic flaws, failures
item.description: DEATH in road accidents is tragic case of systemic indifference and failure of successive governments. The Passengers Welfare Association of Bangladesh reports a shocking death toll and injuries on the road in May. In a monthly report, the organisation said that at least 622 people were killed and 1,652 others injured in 613 accidents in May. On May 26, at least 15 people......

item.title: Power sector’s weak start under BNP
item.description: THE people of Bangladesh are all too familiar with the deceptive nature of sugar-coated political manifestos, which remain in discussion up until election time and are only to be shelved the moment a party takes office after the election, replaced by policies that often turn out to be deeply anti-people. In a country where commerce, industry, and governance are......

item.title: Health risks in changing campuses
item.description: WALK into almost any university canteen in Dhaka and a familiar scene unfolds. Students sit for long hours over laptops, skip proper meals, rely on inexpensive fried snacks and sugary drinks between classes and often stretch their study schedules deep into the night. This is not an unusual or exceptional pattern; it is the everyday rhythm of campus life. Yet behind this......

item.title: Navigating new US tariff offensive
item.description: ON JUNE 2, the Office of the United States Trade representative proposed the imposition of additional tariffs on imports from 60 countries, including Bangladesh, under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974 on the grounds that the targeted states have not undertaken adequate measures to curb forced labour. As a result, exports from Bangladesh to the US may face an......

item.title: Iranian-Americans vow to protest World Cup game in Los Angeles
item.description: When Iran takes to the field on Monday for the first game of its World Cup, Sara Barahman will be among the thousands of protesters outside the stadium waving a very specific flag: the one used by the country before the 1979 Islamic Revolution....

item.title: World Cup teams blast UEFA chief over 'uninteresting' matches remark
item.description: A group of 13 World Cup teams on Sunday hit out at UEFA President Aleksander Ceferin over reported remarks in which he said this year's expanded tournament would lead to many ‘completely uninteresting’ matches....

item.title: Curacao thrashing will boost Germany's 'self confidence'
item.description: Julian Nagelsmann said Germany's 7-1 win against World Cup debutants Curacao on Sunday will grow his charges' self-belief as they seek a fifth title....

item.title: Japan fight back in 2-2 Dutch thriller
item.description: Japan scored an 88th-minute equaliser as they twice fought back to rescue a 2-2 draw with the Netherlands in front of 69,285 in Texas on Sunday to open their World Cup campaigns....

item.title: Yamal fit but will not start Spain's World Cup opener
item.description: Spain coach Luis de la Fuente confirmed that Lamine Yamal is fit but will begin the European champions' World Cup opener against Cape Verde on the bench....

item.title: In crisis-hit Cuba, World Cup offers brief respite
item.description: ‘You need to be happy,’ reads graffiti spray-painted on a ruined building swamped in trash in Cuba's capital Havana. And for 100 minutes, as Morocco held local favorites Brazil to a draw, Cubans complied and put their troubles aside as football's World Cup came to state television on Saturday -- two days late. ...

item.title: Iranian football legend Azizi puts peace above politics amid World Cup tensions
item.description: Iranian football legend Khodadad Azizi, who played in the first World Cup meeting between Iran and the United States in 1998, has told AFP that football should promote ‘peace’ and remain separate from politics....

item.title: Brazil's frailties laid bare in shaky World Cup opener
item.description:
Brazil's defensive frailties, lack of creativity and problems at full-back were exposed in a 1-1 draw with Morocco in their World Cup opener, raising doubts over their chances of lifting a sixth title.
...

item.title: World Cup history beckons for ageless Messi
item.description: Lionel Messi could have signed off from World Cup football on the ultimate high in 2022 but he is back and poised to become the first man to play in six tournaments....

item.title: Germany hammer World Cup debutants Curacao
item.description: Germany won their first opening match at a World Cup since lifting the trophy in 2014 as they eased to a 7-1 win against debutants Curacao in their opening Group E match in Houston on Sunday....

item.title: UGC chair for ethics, integrity in engineering education
item.description: Chairman of the University Grants Commission of Bangladesh Professor Mamun Ahmed on Sunday said that the goal of engineering students should not be only to acquire talent and technical skills, they should also develop as honest, moral and responsible human resources....

item.title: DU celebrates Russian Language Day, Pushkin birth anniv
item.description: The University of Dhaka organised a programme to celebrate the Russian Language Day and the 227th anniversary of birth of modern Russian poet Alexander Pushkin at the Nabab Nawab Ali Chowdhury Senate Bhaban of the university on Sunday....

item.title: Five day entrepreneurship dev training prog inaugurated at BRUR
item.description: A five-day training programme titled Entrepreneurship Development was inaugurated at the Begum Rokeya University, Rangpur on Saturday....

item.title: JS forms standing committees on finance, law ministries
item.description: The Jatiya Sangsad on Sunday formed two parliamentary standing committees on the finance ministry and the law, justice and parliamentary affairs ministry during the sixth sitting of the second session of the 13th parliament....

item.title: Ganosamhati wants complete implementation of FY2026-27 budget
item.description: The Ganosamhati Andolan on Sunday said that the party wanted the complete implementation of the proposed budget for the 2026-27 financial year....

item.title: CUET holds 165th academic council meeting
item.description: The Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology held the 165th meeting of the academic council at CUET in Chattogram on Sunday....

item.title: RUB holds workshop on Kodaly method
item.description: A workshop titled Kodaly Method and Global Music Communication was held at the initiative of the music department of Rabindra University, Bangladesh at Shahzadpur in Sirajganj on Sunday....

item.title: Youths portray raw visuals of contemporary times
item.description: Young artists, mostly female, portray raw visuals of contemporary times through their artworks at a group art exhibition titled ‘A Thousand Tales’ at Safiuddin Shilpalay in the capital’s Dhanmondi area....

item.title: JU student suspended over harassment of female student
item.description: Jahangirnagar University authorities suspended a student accused of harassing a female student aboard a university bus after she had confronted him for allegedly taking her photograph without consent, officials said on Saturday....

item.title: Coast Guard recovers grenades, bullets at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard recovered 10 fresh grenades, 28 rounds of fresh bullets and seized 2kg of hemp in an operation at Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Saturday night....
